taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Software Engineering, Backend Software Engineer
core_skills: Full Stack Development, Modern web development frameworks, Programming languages, Databases, Cloud platforms
skill_match_scores: Full Stack Development:0.0, Modern web development frameworks:1.0, Programming languages:1.0, Databases:1.0, Cloud platforms:1.0
must_have_quals: Proficiency in front-end, back-end development and infrastructure, including experience with modern web development frameworks, programming languages, and databases, Familiarity with cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP) and experience in developing and deploying applications in a cloud-native environment, Knowledge of ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes and experience in building data pipelines to integrate and process diverse data sources, Familiarity with integrating Large Language Models (LLMs) and building agentic workflows, Strong analytical and problem-solving skills to understand complex challenges and devise effective solutions, Excellent interpersonal and communication skills to effectively collaborate with cross-functional teams, stakeholders, and customers, Willingness to embrace new technologies, learn new skills, and adapt to defining and evolving project requirements
preferred_quals: Understanding of containerization (e.g., Docker) and container orchestration (e.g., Kubernetes) is a plus, Understanding of data modeling, data warehousing, and data governance principles, Understanding of prompt engineering, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and agent orchestration is beneficial, Ability to think critically, identify root causes, and propose innovative approaches to overcome technical obstacles, Ability to clearly articulate technical concepts to non-technical audiences and foster a collaborative work environment, Ability to quickly grasp and apply new concepts and stay up-to-date with emerging trends in software engineering
core_responsibilities: Design and implement scalable backend systems for Federal customers using cloud-native AI infrastructure, Build features for agentic systems including multi-layered guardrails and data retrieval optimization, Develop data pipelines and machine learning infrastructure to make data sources accessible by agents, Collaborate with cross-functional teams to execute backend solutions for secure environments, Participate in customer engagements to understand requirements and deliver technical solutions, Define requirements with stakeholders and implement features until they are accepted, Contribute to the platform roadmap and product strategy for the Federal business
job_summary: Scale AI is a company focused on developing reliable AI systems for critical decisions. The Public Sector Software Engineer will design and implement scalable backend systems for federal customers, collaborate with cross-functional teams, and contribute to the platform roadmap and product strategy for the Federal business.
